- Error rates: no increase in 5xx, timeouts, or client cancellations beyond normal variance (SRE to define exact thresholds per pool).
- Fairness/progress:
  - Long-class starvation counters remain at 0 (or below agreed tolerance) and time-in-queue for long does not show unbounded growth.
  - Dispatch share for long remains above minimum floor (config-dependent).
- Stability:
  - No increase in GPU instability symptoms: kernel selection thrash signals, excessive cache eviction spikes, or “oscillating batch shape” patterns (per profiling guidance).

Dedicated success criteria (if used)
- p95 latency reduction on the customer’s reported workload mix, with no SLO violations and no starvation signals.

8) Monitoring checklist (during ramp)
Dashboards
- Scheduler class metrics dashboard (per-class queue depth, time-in-queue, starvation counters, dispatch share).
- Route/model latency (p50/p95/p99) split by shard (canary vs control) where available.
- TTFT dashboard for chat routes (esp. short-class).
- GPU utilization + tokens/sec + batch size distribution.
- Error/timeout/cancellation rate.

What to look for (early warning)
- Long queue depth increasing monotonically while short queue remains stable.
- Starvation counter increments or long time-in-queue p95 trending upward without recovery.
- Sudden oscillations in batch composition leading to latency spikes (possible kernel selection instability).
- Increased cache eviction / KV cache compaction spikes correlated with mixed traffic.

9) Rollback criteria (hard triggers)
Immediate rollback (flag off + revert params) if any occur for > 10 minutes (unless SRE explicitly overrides due to known external cause):
- p95 end-to-end latency regression >= 15% vs control shard for key Hosted API routes/models in the target pool.
- TTFT p95 regression >= 10% for short-class traffic.
- Any sustained starvation signal:
  - starvation counter increments above 0 in production on the target pool, OR
  - long time-in-queue p95 exceeds baseline by >= 25% and is trending upward.
- Error budget risk:
  - 5xx/timeout rate crosses SRE-defined threshold or triggers paging.
- Incident-level symptoms (GPU resets, widespread request failures) correlated with enablement.

Soft rollback / hold (pause ramp, investigate) if:
- Throughput regression 3–5% sustained for > 30 minutes.
- Mixed results: p95 improves but p99 worsens; require review by Runtime TL + SRE before proceeding.

Rollback procedure (summary)
- Flip feature flag off for the canary shard(s).
- Revert any tuned parameters to baseline defaults.
- Confirm recovery on primary dashboards for at least 30 minutes.
- Post incident/rollback note in #eng-releases thread with timestamps, what changed, and next steps.

10) Ownership / staffing
Roles
- Rollout driver: Rafael Mendes
- Runtime decision maker (policy/params): Lena Fischer
- Runtime on-call escalation: Sofia Ivanova
- SRE on-call sponsor: Sean Gallagher
- SLO/alert monitoring: Aisha Bello
- Dashboards/metrics: Allison Grant (with Julian Moore for runtime metric correctness)
- Config/flag plumbing: Benji Okafor
- Release coordination: Connor O’Brien

Coverage plan (proposed)
- Step 1/2 ramps must occur during business hours PT/ET overlap.
- Step 3 overnight hold is allowed only if:
  - SRE paging thresholds are set, and
  - a named driver is available for the first 2 hours after ramp.

Comms
- Single rollout thread in #eng-releases with checklist and links.
- Cross-post key updates to #eng-runtime and #eng-sre.

11) Open questions / TODOs
- Confirm final config keys + safe bounds (Benji).
- Confirm exact target pools and shard mechanism (Rafael + Logan).
- Decide whether Dedicated is in-scope for this first canary (Lauren + Sean + Sofia).
- Finalize pass/fail thresholds with PM stakeholders (Nikhil/Lauren) and SRE error budget policy.
- Attach dashboard links + query IDs once created.

12) Appendix: quick links to collect before canary
- Perf-canary run(s): mixed workload job results (nightly).
- Benchmark-lab comparison report for runtime-1.18 candidate vs baseline.
- Baseline production snapshot: p95/TTFT/throughput for target pools (24h).
- Slack threads: rollout coordination + any known issues/edge case notes.

Change log
- 2025-11-26: initial draft created.
- 2025-12-02: updated rollback criteria and ramp schedule based on starvation edge-case fix and SRE feedback in #eng-sre thread.

source.title: KMS failure modes test matrix (SRE) — hardening + rotation initiative
source.content:
Purpose
- Enumerate KMS dependency failure modes and validate expected system behavior + observability.
- Track test coverage status across CI integration tests, staging fault injection, and game day scenarios.
- Keep this “sheet” aligned with wrapper policy (ADR-007) and on-call runbook.

Scope
- Components: control plane (key metadata/version lookups, config flags), data plane (decrypt/envelope ops), private installer preflight checks.
- KMS providers: generic interface. Some injection methods are AWS-centric but should map to GCP/Azure equivalents.
- Non-goals: cryptographic algorithm changes; pure app-level resilience and operational correctness.

Legend
- Coverage types:
  - U = unit tests (library-level)
  - IT = integration tests (CI/staging harness; controlled failures)
  - CHAOS = staging fault injection (network/latency/throttle)
  - GD = game day scenario (coordinated multi-service)
- Status:
  - Planned = not implemented
  - In progress = PR open / partial
  - Covered = implemented + passing
  - Needs validation = present but not confident / flaky / missing assertions

Assumptions / expected global behavior (from ADR-007 + SRE review notes)
- Hard timeouts on KMS calls, bounded retries with jitter; retry budgets must not amplify KMS load.
- Circuit breaker engages on sustained failure (timeouts/throttling/5xx) and enters degrade mode.
- Degrade mode behavior:
  - For decrypt of existing persisted ciphertext: fail closed (do not serve incorrect plaintext); return actionable error (no infinite retries).
  - For non-critical paths (e.g., background key metadata refresh): fail open where safe (cache + backoff), and never block request threads indefinitely.
- Observability requirements:
  - Metrics: latency histogram + error counter with canonical taxonomy (AccessDenied, NotFound, Throttling, Timeout, InvalidCiphertext, DependencyUnavailable/5xx)
  - Traces: span per KMS operation with provider + operation + error category tags
  - Logs: structured fields include kms_provider, kms_operation, kms_key_id (redacted/hashed as needed), error_category, request_id/correlation_id

Matrix (primary)
Columns:
ID | Failure mode | Where it manifests | Injection / repro method | Expected behavior (app) | Expected signals (metrics/logs/traces) | Alert expectation | Coverage (U/IT/CHAOS/GD) | Status | Owner | Notes / gaps

FM-01 | Hard timeout (single call) | Data plane decrypt, control plane key lookup | Inject sleep > client timeout in KMS stub OR iptables drop to KMS endpoint | Enforce per-call timeout; bounded retries; request returns within end-to-end budget; no thread pool exhaustion | error_category=Timeout; latency bucket hits timeout cap; trace shows retries <= budget | No page on 1-off; warning if elevated | IT, CHAOS | Covered | Sahana Iyer | Assert “retry amplification” not present (see FM-02)
FM-02 | Retry storm / retry amplification | Data plane under load | Increase request QPS + inject 30–50% timeouts; verify global retry budget | Circuit breaker trips; backpressure engages; error rate increases but KMS QPS does not grow superlinearly | retries_total bounded; circuit_breaker_open metric; request shed/backpressure metric | Page if breaker open > N min + customer impact | GD, CHAOS | In progress | Rafael Mendes | Near-miss in us-east referenced in incidents thread; validate tuned jitter/backoff
FM-03 | Throttling (429 / provider throttles) | Data plane + control plane | Configure KMS quota low in staging OR stub returns Throttling | Honor Retry-After if provided; exponential backoff + jitter; do not hammer KMS; prefer serving from safe caches when allowed | error_category=Throttling; throttle_budget/backpressure signals; trace shows backoff | Page only if sustained + 5xx/customer impact | IT, CHAOS, GD | Needs validation | Sean Gallagher | Link to Jira SUP-19377; add assertion that client respects throttle budget
FM-04 | AccessDenied (IAM/policy misconfig) | Installer preflight; runtime decrypt | Remove decrypt permission from runtime principal OR use wrong role | Fail fast; actionable error with missing permission hint; no retries beyond minimal | error_category=AccessDenied; logs include principal/role name (where safe); installer output includes remediation | Ticket/ops alert (non-page) for new deploy; page only if prod regression | IT | Covered | Hanae Suzuki | Ensure no sensitive ARN leakage in customer-visible errors
FM-05 | Wrong key ID / key ARN not found | Installer preflight; control plane | Set key_id to non-existent; stub NotFound | Fail fast; clear message “key not found / wrong region”; no retries > minimal | error_category=NotFound; log includes normalized key_id hash | No page; blocks deployment | IT | Planned | Tomáš Novák | Add to installer validation report output
FM-06 | Key disabled | Runtime decrypt/encrypt; control plane | Disable key in provider; stub “DisabledException” | Fail closed; surface “key disabled” and operator steps; do not fallback to unrelated key | error_category=KeyDisabled (maps to AccessDenied/DependencyUnavailable depending provider); audit log event if config changed | Page if impacts prod traffic | IT, GD | In progress | Ethan Park | Decide taxonomy mapping; ensure runbook has exact provider message mapping
FM-07 | Key scheduled for deletion / pending deletion | Runtime decrypt | Schedule deletion in staging or stub PendingDeletion | Fail closed; error explicitly calls out pending deletion; guidance to cancel deletion or rotate | error_category=KeyPendingDeletion; logs + trace | Page if production | Planned | Tomáš Novák | Add runbook entry; common enterprise audit question
FM-08 | InvalidCiphertext (wrong key material / mismatched key version) | Runtime decrypt during rotation or misconfig | Attempt decrypt ciphertext with wrong key version; simulate dual-read misconfiguration | Fail closed; return deterministic error; trigger rollback criteria if rate exceeds threshold | error_category=InvalidCiphertext; “decrypt_mismatch” counter; rotation-cutover audit events | Page if above rollback threshold | IT, GD | In progress | Rafael Mendes | Must be tied to ADR-008 dual-read window tests
FM-09 | Stale cache serving old key metadata | Control plane + data plane key refresh | Force cache TTL too long; rotate key; observe continued use of old metadata | Cache semantics prevent stale material; refresh on rotation events; safe fallback to dual-read where configured | cache_hit vs refresh metrics; log “stale_key_metadata_prevented” | No page; should be caught in rollout checks | IT | Covered | Nadia Rahman | Validate this in staging with real rotation flags
FM-10 | KMS regional outage / endpoint unreachable | Data plane | Block DNS/egress to kms.<region>; simulate full outage | Circuit breaker opens; requests fail within latency cap; no cascading failures; on-call has clear signals | error_category=DependencyUnavailable/Timeout; breaker open; healthcheck endpoint fails | Page if sustained + prod | CHAOS, GD | Planned | Sean Gallagher | Must coordinate with Observability pack dashboard panels
FM-11 | Elevated KMS latency (no errors) | Data plane | Add 300–800ms delay but keep responses OK | Tail latency for app bounded via timeouts; if within timeout, serve; alerting detects p95/p99 drift | latency histogram shifts; no error spike; traces show dependency time | Warning first; page only if SLO breach | CHAOS | Needs validation | Allison Grant | Alert noise risk (see slack thread about noisy latency alerts)
FM-12 | Partial outage (some KMS operations fail, others OK) | Control plane vs data plane | Stub: Decrypt fails but DescribeKey works | App behavior should match per-op policy; avoid assuming health from DescribeKey only | per-operation metrics differentiate | Alert based on decrypt errors, not general “KMS up” | IT | Planned | Caleb Johnson | Ensure healthcheck endpoint reflects relevant ops
FM-13 | Permission revoked mid-flight (token/role change) | Data plane | Rotate IAM role; revoke grants; long-lived pods continue | Detect AccessDenied quickly; no infinite retries; provide remediation hint | AccessDenied counters increase; audit log notes principal change if applicable | Page if prod-wide | CHAOS | Planned | Jordan Lee | Include Terraform role separation considerations
FM-14 | KMS returns internal 5xx | Data plane | Stub internal error; provider outage | Retry with jitter within budget; circuit breaker on sustained failures | error_category=DependencyUnavailable; breaker open | Page if sustained | IT, CHAOS | In progress | Rafael Mendes | Tie into breaker thresholds
FM-15 | KMS request too large / invalid request (client bug) | Data plane | Force invalid parameters or oversize payload | Fail fast; no retries; treated as non-retriable | error_category=InvalidRequest; logs include operation + sanitized reason | No page; should be caught in CI | U, IT | Planned | Sahana Iyer | Ensure error mapping is consistent with taxonomy ticket
FM-16 | Installer: wrong region / partition mismatch | Private installer | Use key in different region than deployment; enforce region pinning | Installer fails with actionable error; suggests correct region/key | installer validation report includes “region_mismatch=true” | No page | IT | In progress | Bruno Silva | Coordinate with private installer PR-392
FM-17 | Audit logging missing for rotation/cutover toggles | Control plane | Toggle cutover flags; rotate key version | Audit events emitted (who/what/when); export schema includes events | audit_log_event_count; SIEM export includes event types | No page; compliance blocker | IT | Covered | Kevin Osei | Ensure exporter schema version pinned for Private
FM-18 | Metrics/traces missing tags / inconsistent naming | Observability | Review emitted telemetry; verify naming conventions | Metrics present with required tags; dashboards populate correctly | metric cardinality safe; consistent namespaces | No page; blocks rollout checklist | IT | Covered | Allison Grant | Confirmed by observability-pack PR-844

Secondary matrix (coverage by environment)
- CI (integration harness): FM-01, FM-03, FM-04, FM-08 (partial), FM-09, FM-14
- Staging (fault injection): FM-01, FM-02, FM-03, FM-10, FM-11, FM-14
- Game day (2025Q4): FM-02, FM-03, FM-08, FM-10

Open questions / TODO
- Confirm final error taxonomy label for “KeyDisabled” and “PendingDeletion” (provider-specific mapping). (Owner: Hanae)
- Define paging thresholds for sustained circuit breaker open vs latency-only signals to avoid noise. (Owner: Allison)
- Add explicit assertions that retries are bounded and that KMS QPS does not exceed baseline * (1 + retry_budget). (Owner: Sahana)
- Ensure healthcheck endpoint reflects critical decrypt path, not just a lightweight metadata call. (Owner: Caleb)
- Add a “rollback criteria” section to game day plan: error_category=InvalidCiphertext > X% for Y minutes triggers cutover rollback. (Owner: Rafael)

Notes from incidents/support (for prioritization)
- SUP-19377: intermittent 5xx correlated with KMS throttling; customer needed quota guidance + Redwood backoff changes.
- us-east timeout spike thread: near-miss due to retry amplification under partial KMS degradation; motivated stricter retry budgets + jitter.

Change log
- 2025-10-22: initial matrix created (Rafael)
- 2025-11-06: added rotation mismatch / InvalidCiphertext scenarios for dual-read cutover (Rafael)
- 2025-11-14: updated status after integration harness PR; added installer region mismatch scenario (Bruno)
- 2025-11-20: added alert expectation column; marked latency-only alerting as “Needs validation” due to noise concerns (Allison)

FM-04 | AccessDenied (IAM/policy misconfig) | Installer preflight; runtime decrypt | Remove decrypt permission from runtime principal OR use wrong role | Fail fast; actionable error with missing permission hint; no retries beyond minimal | error_category=AccessDenied; logs include principal/role name (where safe); installer output includes remediation | Ticket/ops alert (non-page) for new deploy; page only if prod regression | IT | Covered | Hanae Suzuki | Ensure no sensitive ARN leakage in customer-visible errors
FM-05 | Wrong key ID / key ARN not found | Installer preflight; control plane | Set key_id to non-existent; stub NotFound | Fail fast; clear message “key not found / wrong region”; no retries > minimal | error_category=NotFound; log includes normalized key_id hash | No page; blocks deployment | IT | Planned | Tomáš Novák | Add to installer validation report output
FM-06 | Key disabled | Runtime decrypt/encrypt; control plane | Disable key in provider; stub “DisabledException” | Fail closed; surface “key disabled” and operator steps; do not fallback to unrelated key | error_category=KeyDisabled (maps to AccessDenied/DependencyUnavailable depending provider); audit log event if config changed | Page if impacts prod traffic | IT, GD | In progress | Ethan Park | Decide taxonomy mapping; ensure runbook has exact provider message mapping
FM-07 | Key scheduled for deletion / pending deletion | Runtime decrypt | Schedule deletion in staging or stub PendingDeletion | Fail closed; error explicitly calls out pending deletion; guidance to cancel deletion or rotate | error_category=KeyPendingDeletion; logs + trace | Page if production | Planned | Tomáš Novák | Add runbook entry; common enterprise audit question
FM-08 | InvalidCiphertext (wrong key material / mismatched key version) | Runtime decrypt during rotation or misconfig | Attempt decrypt ciphertext with wrong key version; simulate dual-read misconfiguration | Fail closed; return deterministic error; trigger rollback criteria if rate exceeds threshold | error_category=InvalidCiphertext; “decrypt_mismatch” counter; rotation-cutover audit events | Page if above rollback threshold | IT, GD | In progress | Rafael Mendes | Must be tied to ADR-008 dual-read window tests
FM-09 | Stale cache serving old key metadata | Control plane + data plane key refresh | Force cache TTL too long; rotate key; observe continued use of old metadata | Cache semantics prevent stale material; refresh on rotation events; safe fallback to dual-read where configured | cache_hit vs refresh metrics; log “stale_key_metadata_prevented” | No page; should be caught in rollout checks | IT | Covered | Nadia Rahman | Validate this in staging with real rotation flags
FM-10 | KMS regional outage / endpoint unreachable | Data plane | Block DNS/egress to kms.<region>; simulate full outage | Circuit breaker opens; requests fail within latency cap; no cascading failures; on-call has clear signals | error_category=DependencyUnavailable/Timeout; breaker open; healthcheck endpoint fails | Page if sustained + prod | CHAOS, GD | Planned | Sean Gallagher | Must coordinate with Observability pack dashboard panels
FM-11 | Elevated KMS latency (no errors) | Data plane | Add 300–800ms delay but keep responses OK | Tail latency for app bounded via timeouts; if within timeout, serve; alerting detects p95/p99 drift | latency histogram shifts; no error spike; traces show dependency time | Warning first; page only if SLO breach | CHAOS | Needs validation | Allison Grant | Alert noise risk (see slack thread about noisy latency alerts)
FM-12 | Partial outage (some KMS operations fail, others OK) | Control plane vs data plane | Stub: Decrypt fails but DescribeKey works | App behavior should match per-op policy; avoid assuming health from DescribeKey only | per-operation metrics differentiate | Alert based on decrypt errors, not general “KMS up” | IT | Planned | Caleb Johnson | Ensure healthcheck endpoint reflects relevant ops
FM-13 | Permission revoked mid-flight (token/role change) | Data plane | Rotate IAM role; revoke grants; long-lived pods continue | Detect AccessDenied quickly; no infinite retries; provide remediation hint | AccessDenied counters increase; audit log notes principal change if applicable | Page if prod-wide | CHAOS | Planned | Jordan Lee | Include Terraform role separation considerations
FM-14 | KMS returns internal 5xx | Data plane | Stub internal error; provider outage | Retry with jitter within budget; circuit breaker on sustained failures | error_category=DependencyUnavailable; breaker open | Page if sustained | IT, CHAOS | In progress | Rafael Mendes | Tie into breaker thresholds
FM-15 | KMS request too large / invalid request (client bug) | Data plane | Force invalid parameters or oversize payload | Fail fast; no retries; treated as non-retriable | error_category=InvalidRequest; logs include operation + sanitized reason | No page; should be caught in CI | U, IT | Planned | Sahana Iyer | Ensure error mapping is consistent with taxonomy ticket
FM-16 | Installer: wrong region / partition mismatch | Private installer | Use key in different region than deployment; enforce region pinning | Installer fails with actionable error; suggests correct region/key | installer validation report includes “region_mismatch=true” | No page | IT | In progress | Bruno Silva | Coordinate with private installer PR-392
FM-17 | Audit logging missing for rotation/cutover toggles | Control plane | Toggle cutover flags; rotate key version | Audit events emitted (who/what/when); export schema includes events | audit_log_event_count; SIEM export includes event types | No page; compliance blocker | IT | Covered | Kevin Osei | Ensure exporter schema version pinned for Private
FM-18 | Metrics/traces missing tags / inconsistent naming | Observability | Review emitted telemetry; verify naming conventions | Metrics present with required tags; dashboards populate correctly | metric cardinality safe; consistent namespaces | No page; blocks rollout checklist | IT | Covered | Allison Grant | Confirmed by observability-pack PR-844

Secondary matrix (coverage by environment)
- CI (integration harness): FM-01, FM-03, FM-04, FM-08 (partial), FM-09, FM-14
- Staging (fault injection): FM-01, FM-02, FM-03, FM-10, FM-11, FM-14
- Game day (2025Q4): FM-02, FM-03, FM-08, FM-10

Open questions / TODO
- Confirm final error taxonomy label for “KeyDisabled” and “PendingDeletion” (provider-specific mapping). (Owner: Hanae)
- Define paging thresholds for sustained circuit breaker open vs latency-only signals to avoid noise. (Owner: Allison)
- Add explicit assertions that retries are bounded and that KMS QPS does not exceed baseline * (1 + retry_budget). (Owner: Sahana)
- Ensure healthcheck endpoint reflects critical decrypt path, not just a lightweight metadata call. (Owner: Caleb)
- Add a “rollback criteria” section to game day plan: error_category=InvalidCiphertext > X% for Y minutes triggers cutover rollback. (Owner: Rafael)

Notes from incidents/support (for prioritization)
- SUP-19377: intermittent 5xx correlated with KMS throttling; customer needed quota guidance + Redwood backoff changes.
- us-east timeout spike thread: near-miss due to retry amplification under partial KMS degradation; motivated stricter retry budgets + jitter.

Change log
- 2025-10-22: initial matrix created (Rafael)
- 2025-11-06: added rotation mismatch / InvalidCiphertext scenarios for dual-read cutover (Rafael)
- 2025-11-14: updated status after integration harness PR; added installer region mismatch scenario (Bruno)
- 2025-11-20: added alert expectation column; marked latency-only alerting as “Needs validation” due to noise concerns (Allison)

source.title: On-call Readiness Review (ORR) — Dedicated Autoscaling Bounds + Predictive Mode
source.content:
Doc purpose
- This is the SRE On-call Readiness Review (ORR) checklist for the Dedicated Autoscaling Bounds + Predictive Scaling feature.
- Goal: validate we can safely pilot and GA without creating un-debuggable pages, noisy alerts, or ambiguous ownership.
- This doc is a working checklist; final sign-off captured at the bottom.

Feature summary (what’s launching)
- Dedicated deployments can set customer-configurable autoscaling bounds (min/max replicas or GPU quota).
- Optional predictive mode uses forecasts to bias scale decisions while preserving safety invariants:
  - never exceed max
  - never drop below min
  - reactive signals (saturation/queue depth) override forecast when forecast is wrong, still respecting bounds
- Deterministic evaluation/auditability: record requested desired, bounded desired, and why.

Rollout plan snapshot (SRE view)
- Phase 3: canary rollout in 1 region + limited pilot customers behind per-account/per-deployment feature flags.
- Phase 4: GA contingent on alerts/runbooks validated, error budget policy agreed, and at least one game day completed.
- Default behavior: existing customers unaffected until explicitly enabled.

Owners / escalation
- SRE DRI: Rafael Mendes (on-call readiness, paging, incident response)
- Fleet autoscaler DRI: Vanessa Ortiz
- Autoscaler implementation: Keiko Yamamoto
- Observability: Allison Grant / Sergio Costa
- Product DRI / GA criteria: Lauren Bishop
- Support escalation: Dev Patel

Definitions / terminology (for on-call)
- desired_replicas_requested: autoscaler raw output before policy bounds
- desired_replicas_bounded: desired after applying customer min/max bounds
- bound_hit_max: requested > max (clamped down)
- bound_hit_min: requested < min (clamped up)
- bounded_state_exposed_to_routing: routing sees deployment is at max-bound and may degrade gracefully (without violating bounds)
- unmet_min: situation where min cannot be provisioned due to region capacity shortage; system is in degraded state

Prereqs: artifacts that must exist before pilot/GA
1) Runbook completeness
- [ ] Dedicated autoscaler troubleshooting runbook exists in Confluence and covers:
  - stuck scale-up, stuck scale-down
  - max-bound saturation and sustained queue
  - forecast/prediction pipeline outage or stale forecasts
  - oscillation / thrashing behavior
  - region capacity shortage + unmet min behavior
  - safe mitigations (including feature flag disable, temporary bound changes, manual scale override)
  - escalation paths (Fleet on-call, Control Plane on-call, Observability, Support)

Notes:
- Primary runbook reference: Confluence: eng-infra/runbooks/dedicated-autoscaler-troubleshooting-runbook
- Gap to verify: “unmet min” section should include customer comms template + when to page vs ticket.

2) Dashboards (must be on-call friendly)
- [ ] Grafana dashboard has:
  - desired vs bounded replicas time series (overlay)
  - bound-hit rate (max and min) with customer/deployment filters
  - queue depth and request latency (p50/p95/p99) correlated to scale events
  - prediction vs actual demand with error metrics (and forecast staleness)
  - scale event timeline with reasons (reactive vs predictive vs clamp)
  - “unmet min” status + duration (if applicable)

Notes:
- Dashboard spec: Confluence: eng-platform/dashboards-and-alerts/autoscaling-bounds-observability-dashboard-spec
- Implementation PRs: observability-pack/pr-1934 + pr-1941
- Action: confirm filters do not blow up label cardinality (customer_id/deployment_id use internal IDs; no free-form strings).

3) Alerting (page quality + routing)
- [ ] Paging alerts defined with clear symptoms and thresholds:
  A. Max-bound saturation with sustained queue buildup (customer impact likely)
  B. Unmet min bound (degraded state) for > N minutes
  C. Forecast service stale/unavailable (warning; page only if causing customer impact in pilot)
  D. Autoscaler decision loop unhealthy (no decisions emitted, or errors > threshold)
  E. Oscillation detector (capacity flapping) with latency jitter (warning during pilot; evaluate paging later)

- [ ] Alerts include:
  - runbook link
  - impacted deployment IDs + region
  - current bounded replicas + configured min/max
  - last scale event reason

- [ ] Alert routing:
  - SRE on-call gets customer-impact pages
  - Fleet/Infra secondary for autoscaler issues
  - Observability secondary for dashboard/metrics pipeline issues

Notes:
- Alert updates: Confluence: eng-sre/slo-and-error-budgets/dedicated-autoscaling-slo-and-alerting-updates
- PR: observability-pack/pr-1941-add-alert-rules-max-bound-saturation

4) Auditing / debuggability
- [ ] Audit events exist for:
  - scaling policy changes (who/when/what)
  - autoscaler decisions (requested desired, bounded desired, reasons)
  - feature flag enable/disable per account/deployment

- [ ] Retention and access:
  - enterprise retention meets compliance expectations
  - on-call can query last N audit events quickly (CLI or console view)

Notes:
- PR: redwood/pr-48235-emit-scaling-decision-audit-events
- Internal admin CLI: redwood/pr-48358-add-internal-admin-cli-for-scaling-policy-debug
- On-call checklist item: confirm CLI is documented (install/auth) and works in prod.

5) Feature flags and rollback
- [ ] Feature flags are per-account and per-deployment (no global blast radius).
- [ ] Rollback is one-click (disable predictive mode, or disable bounds feature) and does not require redeploy.
- [ ] Default for existing deployments remains current behavior until opt-in.

Notes:
- Flag PR: redwood/pr-48263-add-feature-flag-per-account-for-predictive-autoscaling
- Migration PR: redwood/pr-48302-update-dedicated-config-migrations-defaults

6) Safety invariants (must be provable / tested)
- [ ] Clamp correctness: never exceed max; never drop below min.
- [ ] Predictor null / missing forecast cannot cause scale-to-zero (verified by regression test).
- [ ] Cooldowns/dampening prevent oscillation under noisy inputs.
- [ ] Reactive override logic is deterministic and auditable.

Notes:
- Hotfix reference: redwood/pr-48321-hotfix-prevent-scale-to-zero-when-predictor-returns-null
- Oscillation guardrails: redwood/pr-48288-add-cooldown-and-anti-oscillation-guardrails


Pilot readiness checklist (what SRE needs before enabling first customer)
A) Pilot scope and comms
- [ ] Pilot customer list approved (PM + SRE + Infra leadership)
- [ ] Support playbook ready for pilot escalations (tags, macros, triage questions)
- [ ] Clear success criteria and stop criteria for pilot

Suggested stop criteria (SRE):
- sustained p95 latency regression > 20% for 30+ minutes attributable to scaling behavior
- oscillation > 4 scale direction changes/hour with correlated latency jitter
- repeated forecast staleness causing missed ramps and queue depth breaches

B) SLO / error budget handling during pilot
- [ ] Error budget policy agreed (pilot-only alerts may be noisy; paging exceptions documented)
- [ ] Canary metrics reviewed after first 24h and 72h

C) Training / handoff
- [ ] On-call handoff posted in #eng-oncall with:
  - runbook link
  - dashboard link
  - how to disable predictive mode
  - how to check configured min/max + last bounded decisions

Reference threads:
- #eng-oncall handoff: Slack:eng-oncall/1748976501
- Pilot incident: Slack:incidents/1748969022 (predictive mode temporarily disabled)


Game day plan (required before GA)
Game day objective
- Prove we can detect, triage, and mitigate the top failure modes in < 15 minutes, and fully resolve/restore within acceptable time.

Required participants
- Driver: Rafael Mendes (SRE)
- Fleet/autoscaler: Vanessa Ortiz or Keiko Yamamoto
- Observability: Sergio Costa
- Control plane/policy: Ethan Park or Jordan Lee
- Support observer: Dev Patel (optional but recommended)

Scenarios (minimum set)
1) Forecast outage / stale forecasts
- Inject: forecast provider returns stale timestamp or times out
- Expected behavior: predictive mode falls back to reactive; audit event emitted; warning alert (non-page unless impact)
- Verify: no unsafe scale down; bounded desired still respects min/max

2) Sudden traffic ramp (predictive helps)
- Inject: load ramp over 10–15 minutes
- Expected: scale-up begins before queue depth breaches (if forecast present); if forecast wrong, reactive catches up; no thundering herd
- Verify: p95 latency stays within pilot SLO; scale events show reason codes

3) Max-bound saturation with queue buildup
- Inject: traffic that exceeds max capacity
- Expected: bounded desired clamped at max; routing sees bounded state; alert fires with runbook link and context
- Verify: page is actionable (contains min/max, bounded replicas, queue depth, region)

4) Unmet min during region capacity shortage
- Inject: simulate scheduler failing to provision min due to capacity shortage
- Expected: deployment enters degraded/unmet_min state; alert fires; runbook instructs comms and mitigation
- Verify: no silent failure; customer-visible status is accurate

5) Oscillation / noisy signals
- Inject: periodic bursts around decision boundary; vary queue depth noise
- Expected: cooldown/dampening reduce flaps; oscillation detector triggers warning; mitigation documented

Artifacts
- Game day tracking: Linear:ENG-22154
- Notes should include: timeline, decisions, what was confusing, what to fix before GA.


Launch gating items (GA go/no-go)
These must be green (or explicitly waived with exec sign-off).

Gating checklist
1) Runbook
- Status: [IN REVIEW]
- Gate: runbook covers all P0/P1 symptoms and has verified steps + links.

2) Alerts
- Status: [IN REVIEW]
- Gate: at least one alert test per rule (synthetic or controlled load) with screenshot + sample page payload in notes.

3) Dashboards
- Status: [MOSTLY READY]
- Gate: on-call can answer within 5 minutes:
  - are we clamped at max?
  - are we meeting min?
  - is forecast stale?
  - what reason drove last scaling change?

4) Rollback
- Status: [READY]
- Gate: disable predictive mode and/or disable feature flag restores baseline behavior without redeploy.

5) Incident learnings incorporated
- Status: [PENDING]
- Gate: pilot incident (Slack:incidents/1748969022) action items closed:
  - null prediction safe handling (done via hotfix)
  - add oscillation guardrails (done)
  - improve page context (verify)

6) Ownership and paging policy
- Status: [READY]
- Gate: ownership documented in runbook; paging routes validated in PagerDuty.

7) Customer comms for unmet min
- Status: [PENDING]
- Gate: include comms template + SLA expectations when region shortage prevents meeting min.

Open issues / action items (as of last modified)
- AI-1 (SRE): Add explicit “unmet min” mitigation steps + customer comms template to runbook. Owner: Rafael Mendes. Due: 2025-09-05.
- AI-2 (Observability): Add panel showing forecast staleness and last successful forecast timestamp (per deployment). Owner: Sergio Costa. Due: 2025-09-06.
- AI-3 (Fleet): Confirm clamp reason codes are stable and documented (reactive_override, predictive_bias, clamp_max, clamp_min, unmet_min). Owner: Keiko Yamamoto. Due: 2025-09-04.
- AI-4 (SRE/Infra): Decide paging vs ticket for forecast pipeline degradation during GA (currently warning). Owners: Miles Thornton + Vanessa Ortiz. Due: 2025-09-03.

Sign-off (draft)
- SRE (Rafael Mendes): NOT YET — pending AI-1 + alert test evidence
- Reliability reviewer (Miles Thornton): NOT YET — pending pilot incident action item verification
- Fleet/Infra (Vanessa Ortiz): CONDITIONAL — ready for expanded pilot; GA contingent on unmet_min comms/runbook
- Product (Lauren Bishop): CONDITIONAL — aligns with GA criteria; awaiting SRE green

Appendix: quick triage cheatsheet (for on-call)
If you get paged for latency + queueing on Dedicated with bounds enabled:
1) Check if bounded at max
- If yes: this is expected clamp behavior; treat as capacity exhaustion. Confirm routing degradation behavior and engage Support for customer comms.
2) Check unmet_min
- If unmet_min true: likely region shortage. Engage Infra secondary; follow comms template; consider moving capacity or advising temporary bound adjustment.
3) Check forecast staleness
- If stale: predictive may be ineffective; confirm reactive scaling is active; consider disabling predictive mode for deployment.
4) If oscillation
- Verify cooldown settings; consider increasing cooldown/step sizing (per runbook) and disabling predictive until stable.

End of doc

title: On-call Readiness Review (ORR) — Dedicated Autoscaling Bounds + Predictive Mode

- Goal: validate we can safely pilot and GA without creating un-debuggable pages, noisy alerts, or ambiguous ownership.

Feature summary (what’s launching)
- Dedicated deployments can set customer-configurable autoscaling bounds (min/max replicas or GPU quota).

  - never exceed max

  - reactive signals (saturation/queue depth) override forecast when forecast is wrong, still respecting bounds
- Deterministic evaluation/auditability: record requested desired, bounded desired, and why.

Rollout plan snapshot (SRE view)
